summary: Solo technical founder building ohm — an AI-powered people discovery platform (live in App Store, built in 30 days with Claude Code). Traveling to Singapore to find collaborators, investors, and builders in the AI agent, social technology, and human discovery space. Pre-seed stage, strong vision and working product, needs local network and credibility bridges.
relevant_user_traits: Solo founder with a working product live in the App Store
Technical builder — ships fast using AI-native tools (Claude Code, agent orchestration)
Building in AI agents, people-matching, and social infrastructure — not a generic SaaS
Has a clear thesis: agent-to-agent introduction server for high-stakes human discovery
Scrappy go-to-market instinct — flew to NYC with 100 flyers, sold to strangers
Thinks in product-philosophy terms: resonance, stance-matching, generative mind index
Pre-seed stage, looking for funding path and co-building relationships
Has existing infrastructure: Supabase backend, Render deployment, Cloudflare DNS, API endpoints
irrelevant_or_low_priority_traits: Film/cinematic brand identity and ad production details
Specific UI/UX design philosophy beyond product positioning
Dating app angle of ohm (romantic matching is a secondary use case for this intent)
Website design links and visual inspiration references
Detailed technical architecture (Supabase schema, API keys, internal tooling)
LinkedIn critique as a personal frustration narrative
current_needs: Warm introductions to Singapore-based investors interested in AI infrastructure or social technology
Connections to founders building adjacent products — AI agents, memory systems, identity layers, social graphs
Potential technical co-founder or early engineer who understands agent architectures
Local context: who matters in Singapore's AI/startup ecosystem, what the scene looks like
People who can pressure-test the pitch and product thesis from an Asian market perspective
Potential early adopters or design partners in Singapore's founder/builder community
current_offers: A working product and live infrastructure others can build on or integrate with
Deep thinking on AI-native people discovery — a thesis most haven't articulated yet
Technical execution speed — can prototype and ship collaboratively in days
Willingness to show up in person, have real conversations, and build relationships (not transactional)
A compelling narrative and vision that investors and builders find energizing
Early access and co-design partnership for ohm's agent-to-agent protocol
constraints: Solo founder — no team yet, limited bandwidth for multiple deep engagements simultaneously
Visiting Singapore — time-limited window to build relationships, not a resident
No existing Singapore network — needs introductions, not just names
Credibility is product-and-hustle based, not pedigree-based — needs people who respect that
Product is early — impressive for speed but not yet at scale or revenue
taste_filter: Builders who ship, not people who only advise or theorize
People who think about technology philosophically, not just commercially
Founders and investors comfortable with pre-product-market-fit ambiguity
Preference for people with original thinking over credential-heavy conventional players
Values depth of conversation over breadth of network
Allergic to performative networking — wants real resonance, which is literally what ohm is about
negative_filters: Pure web3/crypto social graph projects with no AI substance
Corporate innovation theater — large company 'innovation labs' without real autonomy
People who only engage with funded/pedigreed founders
Networking-for-networking's-sake connectors with no domain depth
Investors who require significant traction metrics at pre-seed
Surface-level AI wrapper builders with no thesis on human interaction
successful_conversation_definition: A conversation counts as a win if it produces at least one of: (1) a specific warm introduction to someone else in Singapore the user should meet, (2) a substantive exchange about agent-based people discovery that sharpens the product thesis or reveals a blind spot, (3) genuine mutual interest in collaborating — as co-builder, investor, or design partner — with a concrete next step, or (4) local ecosystem context that changes how the user allocates their limited time in Singapore.
reasoning: This intent is about network-building in a specific geography (Singapore) at a specific stage (solo pre-seed founder with working product). The user's film aesthetic, design philosophy, and dating-app narrative are genuine parts of their identity but don't help find the right people in Singapore — what matters is their builder credibility, AI agent thesis, and willingness to engage deeply. The taste filter mirrors ohm's own philosophy: find people through resonance, not credentials. Negative filters exclude the kind of shallow networking ohm was built to replace. Constraints emphasize that as a visiting solo founder, every conversation must be high-leverage — the user can't afford to spread thin across low-signal meetings.
